title: How I Built My macOS-Style Dev Portfolio with React + Tailwind
excerpt: A breakdown of how I recreated macOS windowing, dock behavior, and portfolio storytelling inside a single web app.
content: This portfolio started as an experiment in interface nostalgia, but it turned into a strong exercise in product thinking.
I wanted the UI to feel familiar like macOS, while still behaving like a modern, performant Next.js app.
The hardest part was balancing delight with clarity. Every animation needed to support navigation, not distract from it.

title: How I Organize My Projects for Sanity and Speed
excerpt: A simple project setup I use to keep experiments shippable, folders predictable, and UI work maintainable.
content: I keep projects small on purpose. Clear data layers, consistent file names, and reusable UI building blocks save a lot of pain later.
The goal is not maximum abstraction. It is enough structure that I can move quickly without losing the plot.
When a project grows, the first thing I try to centralize is content and metadata so I stop hardcoding visual decisions.

title: Small UX Details That Make a Big Difference (and How I Implemented Them)
excerpt: From hover timing to window focus states, these details quietly make interfaces feel polished and trustworthy.
content: Most memorable interfaces are not memorable because of giant features. They are memorable because the small interactions feel considered.
Focus rings, spacing rhythm, drag constraints, and subtle easing functions all influence how stable an app feels.
I look for details that lower cognitive friction. If the interface explains itself with motion and layout, users stay in flow.
